title: Customer relationship marketing (CRM)
summary: 9609 AS — CRM purpose, databases, loyalty, personalisation, and retention vs acquisition.
learningObjectives: Explain the purpose of customer relationship marketing.
Describe how CRM databases and software are used.
Analyse loyalty programmes and personalised marketing.
Evaluate benefits and risks of CRM.

simpleExplanation.title: Keeping customers, not just finding them
simpleExplanation.summary: CRM focuses on long-term relationships — using data to retain customers, increase lifetime value, and personalise offers.
steps: Database stores purchase and contact history.
CRM software integrates sales, marketing, and service.

flashcards.back: Build long-term profitable relationships — retention cheaper than acquisition.
Total profit expected from a customer over entire relationship.
Track purchases, preferences, complaints; segment for targeted campaigns.
Encourages repeat purchase; collects data; increases switching costs.
Email with recommended products based on past orders.
Key account management; track contracts, meetings, support tickets.
Data privacy (GDPR), high IT cost, poor data quality, annoying spam.
CRM implements market orientation — responding to individual customer needs.

sections.content: **CRM** (3.1.7) completes **Topic 3.1**. Modern cases mention **apps, loyalty apps, and data analytics** — link CRM to **digital promotion** (3.3.5) and **ethics** (data privacy).

**Customer database** — names, purchase history, communication preferences.

**Loyalty programmes** — points, tiers, exclusive offers.

**Personalised marketing** — tailored emails, product recommendations.

**CRM software** — integrates sales pipeline, service tickets, marketing automation.

items: **Retention** often costs less than **acquisition** — CRM improves lifetime value.
**Cross-selling** — use data to offer complementary products.
**Service recovery** — resolve complaints fast; logged in CRM.
**GDPR:** Consent required for marketing data in many markets.

sections.question: A supermarket introduces a loyalty app tracking purchases and offering personalised coupons. Analyse two benefits and one risk.
sections.solution:
**Benefit 1 — Retention:** Personalised coupons on **frequently bought items** increase repeat visits vs rivals.

**Benefit 2 — Data:** Purchase data reveals **basket trends** for own-label development and **store layout** — informs marketing and operations.

**Risk — Privacy:** Customers may distrust **data collection**; breach or misuse damages brand; must comply with **data protection** laws and offer opt-out.
